# Page 3^page=3
3 European foreword This document (prEN 18223:2025) has been prepared by Technical Committee CEN/TC "Digital product passport – Framework and system", the secretariat of which is held by DIN. This document is currently submitted to the CEN Enquiry. This document has been prepared under a standardization request addressed to CEN by the European Commission. The Standing Committee of the EFTA States subsequently approves these requests for its Member States. For relationship with EU Legislation, see informative Annex ZA, which is an integral part of this document.

# Page 4^page=4
prEN 18223:2025(en) 4 Introduction 0.1 Organizational interoperability Organizational interoperability should enable the following: — easy integration into existing technology frameworks; — human and machine (software) access. 0.2 Semantic interoperability Semantic interoperability should enable the following: — allow data structuring with standardized or established data schema languages; — enable the usage and re-usage of known ontologies or context namespaces; — avoid sector specific ontologies – allow parallel usage of different ontologies issued by the European Commission in delegated acts with a horizontal layer as general as possible (technology, ontology and domain agnostic). 0.3 Technical interoperability Serialization of data with standardized data format.

# Page 5^page=5
5 1 Scope The scope of this document includes: — the semantic description of a product, including its properties where relevant and the semantic aspects to represent the product lifecycle; — a common information model allowing for the implementation of data dictionary systems; — metadata models and formats to be used in exchange and representation, allowing for the integration of dictionaries; — rules on how to systematically use such metadata models when developing product group specific data models and dictionaries; — technical and organizational interoperability. This document follows the approach of standard interoperability layers and proposes the following aspects in this regard. 2 Normative references The following documents are referred to in the text in such a way that some or all of their content constitutes requirements of this document. For dated references, only the edition cited applies. For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments) applies. ISO 639:2023, Code for individual languages and language groups ISO 8601-1:2019/Amd 1:2022, Date and time — Representations for information interchange — Part 1: Basic rules — Amendment 1: Technical corrections EN ISO 3166-1:2020, Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions - Part 1: Country code (ISO 3166-1:2020) prEN 18219, Digital product passport - Unique identifiers prEN 18219, Digital product passport - Unique identifiers prEN 18221, Digital product passport - data storage, archiving, and data persistence prEN 18239, Digital Product Passport - access rights management, information system security, and business confidentiality 3 Terms and definitions For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply. ISO and IEC maintain terminology databases for use in standardization at the following addresses: — ISO Online browsing platform: available at http://www.iso.org/obp — IEC Electropedia: available at http://www.electropedia.org/

# Page 6^page=6
prEN 18223:2025(en) 6 3.1 interoperability capability of two or more entities to exchange items in accordance with a set of rules and mechanisms implemented by an interface in each entity, in order to perform their specified tasks [SOURCE: Error! Reference source not found., 3.12] 3.2 semantic interoperability ability for data shared by systems to be understood at the level of fully defined domain concepts [SOURCE: Error! Reference source not found.] 3.3 technical interoperability ability that covers the applications and infrastructures linking systems and services 3.4 digital product passport DPP digital record of product characteristics throughout its life cycle Note 1 to entry: Example characteristics include environmental sustainability, environmental impact, and recyclability 3.5 digital product passport id DPP ID unique identifier of an instance of the digital product passport 4 Semantic interoperability 4.1 Semantic model 4.1.1 General This standard uses UML class diagrams Error! Reference source not found. to illustrate the core concepts of a product and DPP, their data, properties and their relationships. A JSON representation is provided in Clause 5. If there are discrepancies between the UML diagrams, text and JSON representations the prose text of clause 4, including the tables, is authoritative. 4.1.2 Semantic model of the digital product passport 4.1.2.1 Digital product passport The model presented below in Figure 1 defines what the entities are that compose a digital product passport instance and the data points it is required to include.

# Page 7^page=7
7 Figure 1 — UML representation of the entities of the semantic model of the DPP. Figure 1 presents the base semantic model of a digital product passport. The digital product passport is modelled through the class DigitalProductPassport as a digital container that contains zero to manyDataElements. The model is structured based on the following principles: — DataElement is an abstract class. It represents the generic concept of a data point. As an abstract class, it is the common superclass for all other classes, and it is never used directly in a DPP instance. — DataElementCollection: A concrete subclass of DataElement (see 4.1.2.3) that acts as a container for a set of other named DataElements. This allows for creating complex structures with mixed data types, as it can contain SingleValuedDataElements (see 4.1.2.5), MultiValuedDataElements (see 4.1.2.6), RelatedResource (see 4.1.2.7), and other nested DataElementCollections (see 4.1.2.4). — SingleValueDataElement: This is the simplest concrete subclass of DataElement. It represents a single unit of information (see 4.1.2.5). — MultiValuedDataElement: A concrete subclass of DataElement that contains an ordered list of other DataElements, all of which shall be of the same type. For example, it could contain a list of SingleValueDataElements (like an array of numbers) or a list of DataElementCollections (like an array of similar objects) (see 4.1.2.4). — Other Concrete subclasses: MultiLanguageDataElement (see 4.1.2.8) and RelatedResource (see 4.1.2.7) are other specialized subclasses of DataElement, used for representing a value in multiple languages or linking to an external resource, respectively.

# Page 8^page=8
prEN 18223:2025(en) 8 The following data points (see Table 1) shall be published as attributes of a digital product passport instance: Table 1 — Attributes of the DigitalProductPassport class Attribute name Description DataType Cardinality digitalProductPassportId unique identifier of an instance of the digital product passport. String [1] uniqueProductIdentifier unique string of characters for the identification of a product, that also enables a web link to the digital product passport String formatted according to prEN 18219 [1] granularity level of granularity of the uniqueProductIdentifie r Enumeration according to 4.1.2.2 [1] dppSchemaVersion reference standard the DPP instance schema refers to String [1] dppStatus status of the DPP instance as digital resource String [1] lastUpdate date and time of the latest update to the DPP instance String formatted as Timestamp UTC- based according to ISO 8601-1:2019/Amd 1:2022 [1] economicOperatorId unique string of characters for the identification of an actor involved in a product’s life cycle String formatted according to prEN 18219 [1] facilityId unique string of characters for the identification of locations or buildings involved in a product’s value chain or used by actors involved in a product’s life cycle String formatted according to prEN 18219 [0..1] contentSpecificationIds reference to horizontal or product-type related content specifications for the DPP String [0..*] {DataElement} data elements of DPP DataElement (abstract) as per 4.1.2.3 [0..*]

# Page 9^page=9
9 EXAMPLE Example values for the “dppStatus” attribute are: — Active, — Inactive, — Archived, — Invalid Any further enumeration value provided by relevant legal acts. The digitalProductPassportId shall be globally unique and should be based on a URI/URL structure. {DataElement} is a placeholder for additional content or other attributes of the DPP. The notation {<name>} means that the naming is not fixed. This includes data points mentioned in ESPR 0, Annex III and not included in Table 1. 4.1.2.2 Enumeration Some of the attributes required by the DigitalProductPassport class are enumerations whose values are predetermined. The values allowed for the “granularity” attribute are: — Model, — Batch, — Item. 4.1.2.3 DataElement The DataElement is an abstract class which is the base class for its concrete subclasses: DataElementCollection (4.1.2.4) SingleValuedDataElement (4.1.2.5), MultiValuedDataElement (4.1.2.6), RelatedResource (4.1.2.7) and MultiLanguageDataElement (4.1.2.8). It specifies the following attributes (see Table 2). Table 2 — Definition of DataElement attributes Attribute Name Description Datatype Cardinality elementId The relative identifier of the DataElement shall be unique within its location (i.e. in the DPP or in the level of DataElementCollection or MultiValuedDataEleme nt) and it is recommended to be unique in the DPP instance. The corresponding absolute path of the data String [1]

# Page 10^page=10
prEN 18223:2025(en) 10 Attribute Name Description Datatype Cardinality element shall be unique within the DPP instance. dictionaryReference The reference to the unique identifier of the data point specification defined in the repository/data dictionary String [0..1] 4.1.2.4 DataElementCollection The DataElementCollection is a collection of DataElements included in the digital product passport entity. It contains at least one DataElement. This class inherits the attributes elementId and dictionaryReference from the abstract DataElement class. The DataElementCollection is an object and as such it may contain semantically different objects. A DataElementCollection shall contain at least one instance of a DataElement's subclasses (DataElementCollection, SingleValuedDataElement, MultiValuedDataElement or RelatedResource). 4.1.2.5 SingleValuedDataElement The SingleValuedDataElement represents a single data point with one value. This class inherits the attributes elementId and dictionaryReference from the abstract DataElement class and adds the following specific attributes (see Table 3). Table 3 — Definition of SingleValuedDataElement attributes Name Description Datatype Cardinality Example value Contains the data of the value encapsulated in the DataElement <any JSON datatype> as described in Clause 5 [1] valueDataType Specifies the data type of the value needed for the interpretation of the value data String as described in 4.1.2.9 [0..1] “xsd:integer” 4.1.2.6 MultiValuedDataElement The MultiValuedDataElement is a list which contains similar objects of the same data type. It contains at least one or many DataElements. This class inherits the attributes elementId and dictionaryReference from the abstract DataElement class and adds the following specific attributes (see Table 4).

# Page 11^page=11
11 Table 4 — Definition of MultiValuedDataElement attributes Name Description Datatype Cardinality Example valueDataType Specifies the data type of the value needed for the interpretation of the value data String as described in 4.1.2.9 [0..1] “xsd:integer” [DataElement] data elements of the same type within the multi valued data element DataElement (abstract) as per 4.1.2.3 [0..*] [DataElement] is a placeholder for additional content or other attributes of the MultiValuedDataElement. 4.1.2.7 RelatedResource The RelatedResource is the semantic representation of any instance of a document or certificate that might be required to be included into a digital product passport instance. This class inherits the attributes elementId and dictionaryReference from the abstract DataElement class and adds the following specific attributes (see Table 5). Table 5 — Definition of RelatedResource attributes Name Description Datatype Cardinality Example contentType The IANA Media Type/MIME type. String [1] "application/pdf" url The link to the resource String formatted as URL according to RFC 3986 0 [1] language The language of the resource, if applicable. String formatted as localized language tag with: two (2) characters language code as in ISO ISO 639:2023 (e.g "en"), and two (2) characters country code as in EN ISO EN ISO 3166-1:2020 [0..1] “en-GB” “de-DE” “de-AT” resourceTitle Human readable title of the resource String [0..1] "Maintenance manual" Additionally, it can include other attributes if required by the relevant legal acts.

# Page 12^page=12
prEN 18223:2025(en) 12 4.1.2.8 MultiLanguageDataElement The MultiLanguageDataElement represents a language dependent data point whose value varies with respect to the language associated with it. It contains at least one or manyMultiLanguageValues (see 4.1.1.7.1). This class inherits the attributes elementId and dictionaryReference from the abstract DataElement class. 4.1.2.8.1 MultiLanguageValue The MultiLanguageValue represents a language dependant value. It includes the following metadata attributes (see TTable 6 ). Table 6 — Attributes of the MultiLanguageValue object Name Description Datatype Cardinality Example value Contains the data of the value in the MultiLanguageVal ue String [1] “a product description” language Specifies the language of the value within the MultiLanguageVal ue. String formatted as localized language tag with: two (2) characters language code as in ISO ISO 639:2023 (e.g "en"), and two (2) characters country code as in EN ISO EN ISO 3166-1:2020 [1] “en-gb” “de-de” “de-at” 4.1.2.9 Value data type The valueDataType should allow XSD data types conformant to W3C XML Schema 0 and 0 except: — the XSD data types that represent a list of values within a single whitespace-separated string (i.e., ENTITIES, IDREFS, and NMTOKENS) shall not be supported. — XSD string derived built-in types shall not be supported, and — the following XSD primitive types shall not be supported: NOTATION, QName. — base64 binary may be supported 4.2 Data model requirements to manage DPP changes During its lifetime, the digital product passport may undergo changes that impact the data points to be declared, like for example the environmental performance. These changes could lead to a modification of the DPP initially published for a product, either through a change of an existing property or document, or through the addition of information or a property not in the initial instance. To be properly archived, every digital product passport lifetime event resulting into a change to a DPP should be kept track of. Data include for example:

# Page 13^page=13
13 — identifier (e.g., digitalProductPassportId, uniqueProductIdentifier); — timestamp (when the change occurred); — actor as per prEN 18239 (who made the change); — changed properties ( specific properties modified). Any changes to a DPP shall be logged and archived by the responsible economic operator according to prEN 18221 requirements - delegated acts can define to what extent. 4.3 Model for repositories for the data dictionaries To enable cross-sector semantic interoperability for digital product passport, while avoiding constraints on how DPP information may be defined and ensuring openness to different technologies (or technological approaches), a decoupled approach shall be applied. In this approach, data points and their definitions are managed independently. Each data point should be associated with a machine-readable semantic definition stored in a repository. The governance of such repository should be regulation-based, but shall allow for the inclusion of definitions beyond regulatory requirements where appropriate. Each definition shall have a unique identifier and shall occur only once within a repository to enable unambiguous referencing. This also allows for mapping between definitions contained in different semantic catalogues (repositories). This approach contributes to long-term availability and consistency of definitions, supports multilingual use, and facilitates the management of controlled value lists (e.g. non-numeric values of product properties). It also enables leaner DPP instances by reducing the volume of embedded semantic information. In the DPP semantic model 4.1.2.1, semantic definitions are referenced using the attribute "dictionaryReference". This attribute links a given DataElement to its corresponding definition in the repository. 5 Technical interoperability 5.1 General The definitions in this clause enable the efficient employment of the semantic representation defined in Clause 4 by notified actors, systems and processes involved with the DPP. 5.2 Serialization syntax The serialization of data with standardized data structures shall follow the model provided in Clause 4 . The data syntaxes is defined in Error! Reference source not found.. To serialize the entities defined in Clause 4 , the following guidelines shall be adopted. For compressed serialisation in JSON see examples in 5.2 For expanded full serialisation in JSON see examples Annex A. For serialisation in XML see examples Annex B. 5.2.1 Naming conventions and built-in types The naming conventions adopted are:

# Page 14^page=14
prEN 18223:2025(en) 14 — Pascal case (PascalCase) is used for the UML Classes, example “DataElement”. — Camel case (camelCase) is used for JSON, XML and UML attributes. EXAMPLE “uniqueProductIdentifier” An optimized representation of a Data Element in the conceptual model shall use the elementId as the JSON object key. The other metadata, the dictionaryReference and valueDataType are expected to be defined in external Data dictionaries (see 4.3) and therefore omitted from the operational DPP payload. 5.2.2 JSON built-in types for values JSON native built-in types ( Error! Reference source not found.) should be used to declare values if there is a specification of the data type by a data dictionary or semantic repository. If there is no such specification, then the type “string” in conjunction with the metadata attribute valueDataType may be used. The data type mappings are described in Table 7 Table 7 — Mapping of XSD to JSON Data Types valueDataType(XSD) JSON Type Format / Pattern (RegEx) xsd:string String N/A xsd:boolean Boolean true or false xsd:decimal Number A JSON Number. For high- precision values, a string may be used. xsd:integer Number A JSON Number with no fractional part. xsd:double, xsd:float Number A JSON Number. xsd:date String YYYY-MM-DD (Error! Reference source not found.) xsd:dateTime String YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (Error! Reference source not found.) xsd:anyURI String A valid URI according to RFC 3986 0 xsd:base64Binary String encoded as base64 5.2.3 Serialization of the DigitalProductPassport class A DigitalProductPassport class (4.1.2.1 ) shall be serialized in JSON for the operational data exchange as a JSON object. The following example shows how the header of a Digital Product Passport is serialized. These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. EXAMPLE { //***DPP HEADER*** "digitalProductPassportId": "https://www.example.org/dpp/1234545", "uniqueProductIdentifier": "https://www.example.org/1234545",

# Page 15^page=15
15 "granularity": "Model", "dppSchemaVersion": "ENXXX:v1.0", "dppStatus": "Active", "lastUpdated": "2025-08-22T03:12:00Z", "economicOperatorId": "gxx:ppp456789", "facilityId": "gxx:xxx987654", // optional "contentSpecificationIds": [ "prEN1234_xyz", "prEN5678_abc" ] //***END OF DPP HEADER*** // … rest of the DPP data } 5.2.4 Serialization of a DataElementCollection A DataElementCollection (4.1.2.4 ) is serialized as a JSON object. Its fields may have any necessary combination of data types. In practice, complex nesting should be carefully considered when creating DPP requirements. These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. EXAMPLE DataElementCollection with a combination of different simple and complex types { //...DPP HEADER... "performanceMetrics": { // DataElementCollection "maxPressure": 750.0, // SingleValueDataElements "isFireResistant": true, "efficiencyRatings": [ // MultiValueDataElements 0.95, 0.92, 0.88 ], "complexTraits": [ // additional MultiValueDataElement { // DataElementCollection "fieldOne": 1.0, "fieldTwo": "stringValueOne" }, { // DataElementCollection of same type as the first one "fieldOne": 2.0, "fieldTwo": "stringValueTwo" } ] } } 5.2.5 Serialization of a SingleValuedDataElement SingleValuedDataElements (4.1.2.5 ) are serialized as JSON «key-value pair». In case the SingleValuedDataElement is contained in MultiValueDataElement, the key-value pair may be simplified to a value of a native data type. The following examples demonstrate how SingleValueDataElements are used. These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. EXAMPLE 1 standalone SingleValueDataElements { //...DPP HEADER... "manufacturerName": "ExampleCorp", //type string

# Page 16^page=16
prEN 18223:2025(en) 16 "recycledContentPercentage": 25.5 //type number } EXAMPLE 2 SingleValueDataElements inside a complex data type { //...DPP HEADER... "performanceMetrics": { "maxPressure": 750.0, // as regular key-value pairs "isFireResistant": true, "efficiencyRatings": [ 0.95, // as native values 0.92, 0.88 ] } } 5.2.6 Serialization of a MultiValuedDataElement A MultiValuedDataElement (4.1.2.6 ) is serialized as a JSON array where all items are of the same JSON data type. This type can be a simple native type (like numbers or strings) or a complex type (like objects). When a data element is an item in a JSON array (a MultiValueDataElement), it does not have an explicit key. It is identified by its position (or index) within the array. These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. EXAMPLE 1 Array of native types { //...DPP HEADER… "supportedVoltages":[ 110, 220, 240 ] } EXAMPLE 2 Array of Objects { //...DPPHEADER… "componentParts":[ { "partID":"A-101", "material":"Steel" }, { "partID":"B-202", "material":"Recycled Plastic" } ] }

# Page 17^page=17
17 5.2.7 Serialization of a RelatedResource RelatedResources(4.1.2.7 ) are serialized as JSON objects. These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. EXAMPLE referencing the operating instruction of a product { "resourceTitle": "User Manual for the Smart Thermostat Pro", "contentType": "application/pdf", "url": "https://data.example.com/manuals/thermostat-pro_v2.1.pdf", "language": "en-GB" } 5.2.8 Serialization of a MultiLanguageDataElement MultiLanguageDataElement (4.1.2.8 ) is serialized as JSON array containing JSON objects, each of them serializes a MultiLanguageValue (4.1.2.8.1 ). Each MultiLanguageValue object contains the language dependant value and the corresponding language. These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. EXAMPLE The productDescription provided in multiple languages { //...DPP HEADER... "productDescription": [ { "value": "One Thing", "language": "en-IE" }, { "value": "Una Cosa", "language": "es-ES" }, { "value": "Une Chose", "language": "fr-CH" } ] }

# Page 18^page=18
prEN 18223:2025(en) 18 Annex A (normative) Expanded full data model serialized in JSON These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. Serialization of the DigitalProductPassport class A DigitalProductPassport4.1.2.1 serialized as JSON is shown in 5.2.3 Serialization of a DataElementCollection A DataElementCollection4.1.2.4 serialized as a fully expanded JSON object is shown in Example 1 EXAMPLE 1 DataElementCollection as fully expanded JSON object { //...DPP HEADER... "elements": [ { "elementId": "performanceMetrics", "objectType": "DataElementCollection", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1.eu/performanceMetrics", "elements": [ { "elementId": "maxPressure", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1.eu/maxPressure", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": 2.5 }, { "elementId": "efficiencyRatings", "objectType": "MultiValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRatings", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "elements": [ { "elementId": "efficiencyRating1", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRating", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": 0.95 }, { "elementId": "efficiencyRating2", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRating", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": 0.92 }, { "elementId": "efficiencyRating3", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement",

# Page 19^page=19
19 "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRating", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": 0.88 } ] } ] } ] } DataElementCollection with different dictionary references is shown in Example 2: EXAMPLE 2 DataElementCollection as fully expanded JSON object with different dictionary references { { "elementId": "collectionEconomicOperator", "objectType": "DataElementCollection", "dictionaryReference": "https://organizationDictionary.eu/organization", "elements": […] }, { "elementId": "collectionMaterialsOfConcern", "objectType": "DataElementCollection", "dictionaryReference": "https://materialsDictionary.eu/materials/criticalMaterials", "elements": […] }, { "elementId": "collectionProductSpecific", "objectType": "DataElementCollection", "dictionaryReference": "https://product- sector.eu/productDescription/productClass", "elements": […] } Serialization of a SingleValuedDataElement A SingleValuedDataElement4.1.2.5 serialized as a fully expanded JSON object is shown in Example 3 EXAMPLE 3 SingleValuedDataElement as fully expanded JSON object { "elements": [ { "elementId": "maxPressure", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1/maximumPressure ", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": 2.5 }, { "elementId": "recycledContentPercentage", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https:/dictionary1/recycledContentPercentage", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": 25.5 }

# Page 20^page=20
prEN 18223:2025(en) 20 ] } Serialization of a MultiValuedDataElement A MultiValuedDataElement4.1.2.6 serialized as fully expanded JSON object is shown in Example 4 EXAMPLE 4 MultiValuedDataElement as a fully expanded JSON object { "elements": [ { "elementId": "efficiencyRatings", "objectType": "MultiValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": " https://dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRatings", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": [ { "elementId": "efficiencyRating1", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": " https://dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRating", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": "0.95" }, { "elementId": "efficiencyRating2", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": " https://dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRating", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": "0.92" }, { "elementId": "efficiencyRating2", "objectType": "SingleValuedDataElement", "dictionaryReference": " https://dictionary1.eu/efficiencyRating", "valueDataType": "xsd:float", "value": "0.88" } ] } ] } Serialization of a RelatedResource A RelatedResource4.1.2.6 serialized as a fully expanded JSON object is shown in Example 5 EXAMPLE 5 RelatedResource as a fully expanded JSON object { "elements": [ { "elementId": "userManual", "objectType": "RelatedResource" "dictionaryReference": " https://dictionary1.eu/document/userManual", "language": "en", "resourceTitle": "User Manual for the Smart Thermostat Pro", "contentType": "application/pdf ", "url": "https://data.example.com/manuals/thermostat-pro_v2.1.pdf", } ]

# Page 21^page=21
21 } Serialization of a MultiLanguageDataElement A MultiLanguageDataElement4.1.2.8 serialized as a fully expanded JSON object is shown in Example 6 EXAMPLE 6 MultiLanguageDataElement as a fully expanded JSON object { "elements": [ { "elementId": "productDescription", "objectType": "MultiLanguageDataElement", "dictionaryReference": "https://dictionary1/productDescription", "value": [ { "language": "en-IE", "value": "The description of a product." }, { "language": "gr", "value": "Η περιγραφή ενός προϊόντος." }, { "language": "fr-CH", "value": "Description d'un produit." } ] } ] }

# Page 22^page=22
prEN 18223:2025(en) 22 Annex B (normative) Data model compressed serialisation in XML These examples do not necessarily reflect real values that will be found in a Digital Product Passport. Serialization of the DigitalProductPassport class A DigitalProductPassport4.1.2.1 serialized as XML is shown in Example 1 EXAMPLE 1 DigitalProductPassport serialized as XML <dpp:DigitalProductPassport xmlns:dpp="https://standards.cen.eu/dpp/18223/v1.0/schema" xmlns:specA="https://ec.europa.eu/schemas/prEN1234_xyz/v1.0/schema" xmlns:specB="https://ec.europa.eu/schemas/prEN5678_abc/v1.0/schema"> <dpp:digitalProductPassportId> https://www.example.org/dpp/1234545 </dpp:digitalProductPassportId> <dpp:uniqueProductIdentifier> https://www.example.org/1234545 </dpp:uniqueProductIdentifier> <dpp:granularity>Model</dpp:granularity> <dpp:dppSchemaVersion>prEN18223:v1.0</dpp:dppSchemaVersion> <dpp:dppStatus>Active</dpp:dppStatus> <dpp:lastUpdated>2025-08-22T03:12:00Z</dpp:lastUpdated> <dpp:economicOperatorId>gxx:ppp456789</dpp:economicOperatorId> <dpp:facilityId>gxx:xxx987654</dpp:facilityId> <dpp:contentSpecificationIds> <dpp:item>prEN1234_xyz</dpp:item> <dpp:item>prEN5678_abc</dpp:item> </dpp:contentSpecificationIds> <!-- Additional Data Elements as per specA or specB here --> </dpp:DigitalProductPassport> Serialization of a DataElementCollection A DataElementCollection4.1.2.4 serialized as XML is shown in Example 2 EXAMPLE 2 DataElementCollection serialized as XML <dpp:DigitalProductPassport xmlns:dpp="https://standards.cen.eu/dpp/18223/v1.0/schema" xmlns:specA="https://ec.europa.eu/schemas/prEN1234_xyz/v1.0/schema"> <!-- Begin DPP Header Elements --> <!-- ... End DPP Header Elements --> <specA:performanceMetrics> <specA:maxPressure>750.0</specA:maxPressure> <specA:isFireResistant>true</specA:isFireResistant> <specA:efficiencyRatings> <specA:item>0.95</specA:item> <specA:item>0.92</specA:item> <specA:item>0.88</specA:item>

# Page 23^page=23

# Page 26^page=26
prEN 18223:2025(en) 26 Annex ZA (informative) Relationship between this European Standard and the the ecodesign requirements of Commission Regulation (EU) No 2024/1781 aimed to be covered This European Standard has been prepared under a Commission’s standardization request C(2024) 5423 final of 31.07.2024 to provide one voluntary means of conforming to the ecodesign requirements of Commission Regulation (EU) No 2024/1781 of 28.06.2024 implementing Directive 2009/125/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council with regard to ecodesign requirements for digital product passports in support of Union policy on ecodesign requirements for sustainable products and on batteries and waste batteries Once this standard is cited in the Official Journal of the European Union under that Regulation, compliance with the normative clauses of this standard given in Table ZA.1 confers, within the limits of the scope of this standard, a presumption of conformity with the corresponding ecodesign requirements of that Regulation and associated EFTA regulations. part 4: European standard(s) on interoperability (technical, semantic, organization) Table ZA.1 — Correspondence between this European Standard and Commission Regulation (EU) No 2024/1781 of 28.06.2024 implementing Directive 2009/125/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council with regard to ecodesign requirements and digital product passports in support of Union policy on ecodesign requirements for sustainable products and on batteries and waste batteries and Commission’s standardisation model references repository containing access rights definition on data element level 10.4 Clause 4.1.2 , 4.2 data model and sematic model enable change management, back-up and archiving
